description_long: This is a medium-sized, handwoven picnic basket with a deep, rounded body and a sturdy, braided handle arching over the top. The basket is constructed from interlaced strips that create a visually striking, open-weave pattern, allowing glimpses of the interior. The reddish-brown coloration suggests a natural, possibly stained wicker or rattan material, giving it a rustic and traditional appearance. The handle is thick and intricately braided, securely attached to the rim on both sides, and designed for comfortable carrying. There is no visible lid or closure mechanism, leaving the basket open at the top for easy access to its contents. The overall craftsmanship and finish indicate a utilitarian yet aesthetically pleasing object suitable for carrying food or personal items in parks or urban green spaces.
description: A handwoven, reddish-brown picnic basket with an open top and a thick, braided handle. The basket features a rustic, open-weave pattern and no visible lid.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open top, deep basket body, and the full arch of the braided handle.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness and braiding detail of the handle, with the basket's open weave visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the open top and the basket's rounded shape with the handle arching overhead.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the handle's attachment and the basket's layered weave structure.
attribute_handle_type: Single, fixed, thick braided handle made of the same wicker material, arching from rim to rim.
attribute_weave_pattern: Open, rustic plain weave with visible gaps and a layered, interlaced structure.
